essence: David directed a multi-Mac housekeeping pass: audit per-project trash folders, pre-review every deletion script before running, use du to explain 650GB of disk usage, reconcile Tailscale vs the Start Tailscale app, and check Assembey/agentic-OS alignment with the Ansible config.

actions.text: Audit trash folders across all yard project directories and report file count and total GB
Show the delete-script list for review before running trash cleanup, then run du to explain 650GB used
Research and reconcile Tailscale vs Start Tailscale app usage
Confirm Assembey is Ansible-managed and review agentic OS against the Ansible config for needed updates

loop.label: multi-Mac disk cleanup and Ansible alignment

essence: David decided remote access to his Macs should be visual remote desktop rather than SSH, and the agentic OS must track machine locations because the M2 Mini will later move from the home network to the shop.

actions.text: Update agentic OS config to record machine locations and support remote-desktop access that survives the M2 Mini moving to the shop

essence: While recording takes for a Cowork second-brain video intro (referencing OpenClaw and the Ralph Wiggum iteration loop), David pivoted to agentic-OS work: VPN into the M4 Mini and M2, verify Tailscale is installed, and find the previously written Tailscale/VPS setup documentation.

actions.text: Verify Tailscale is installed on the fleet machines and locate the prior Tailscale/VPS setup documentation

essence: David demanded the lingering untracked git files be committed immediately during the brains repo cleanup session.

actions.text: Do a general check and cleanup of the brains folder root structure and files

essence: David hit appydave-plugin namespace and alias-wiring failures (including reloading the OMI skill), insisted unit tests belong inside tight code-iteration loops since the IR compiler already had 200+ tests on the other system, and pushed back on duplicating data in both project memory and Claude — project memory alone is enough.

actions.text: Wire David's aliases into the appropriate locations so plugin namespace lookups stop failing

essence: David initiated a structured review: list every brain created in the session and turn each one into its own task for a detailed health check.

essence: David scoped an AI workflow POC (WUI) as an isolated, throwaway app built in a worktree on a B2B branch — proper client-server where the server (not the client) talks to the engine and the Claude token authenticator — with optionally human-filled mock answers, the new incident panel, TUI-like output data, and two screens: workflow configuration plus a wizard-style flow-execution UI on the new engine that supports parallel agents and conditional steps.

actions.text: Build the workflow POC isolated in its own folder/worktree on a B2B branch so it can be discarded later
Use client-server architecture with the server handling engine and Claude token authenticator communication

essence: Brief planning checkpoint where David had the agent reread the entire prior conversation to confirm it understood the kind of workflow application he intends to build.

David learned that tmux is only a terminal multiplexer splitting panes, and that the left-menu multi-app log dashboard he keeps seeing in development setups is a process manager / log-aggregation tool like PM2 acting as a control panel for multiple services.

Café conversation in Chiang Mai spanning AI wearable transcription and recording-privacy law (Queensland/WA), Whisper-based dictation with custom dictionaries (how FliVideo gets spelled right), and Thai visa logistics — 60/30-day extensions, 90-day reporting, and David needing to exit Thailand by March 24 for his six-month limit.

Informal group discussion over drinks where Temporal was pitched as the code-based orchestrator for long-running workflows (replacing fragile 40-node Make.com sales automations so an LLM can operate over clean code), alongside the small-model-calls-large-model planning pattern (~20% benchmark lift without fine-tuning) and docs-as-pattern-files with test implementations as API-change canaries.

At a Chiang Mai AI meetup David saw a bilingual (Thai/English) community-platform demo that turns meetup transcripts into a knowledge base plus apps, and probed a home-server agentic stack — LM gateway routing local quantized models, coder models favored for tool-calling, separate Whisper/TTS/embedding models — while wondering whether he can Tailscale his M2/M4 Mac fleet and host local models on it.
